Event: Starts drinking heavily from a very young age
Explanation: Long-term alcoholism severely harms him and offers no benefit to anyone.

Event: Attends AA in Season 7
Explanation: Seeking treatment improves his chances of recovery and reduces harm to others around him.

Event: Falls off the wagon repeatedly
Explanation: Relapsing undermines his own recovery and destabilizes those around him.

Event: Frequently attacks his father when Frank screws up or hurts someone
Explanation: He vents anger and maybe protects others but escalates violence and consequences.

Event: Protects his younger siblings fiercely
Explanation: Being protective costs him effort but significantly helps keep them safe.

Event: Coasts through high school instead of applying himself
Explanation: Underperforming squanders his potential without clearly helping anyone else.

Event: Accepts Mandy applying to colleges on his behalf
Explanation: Letting her push him toward college benefits him and gives others hope for his future.

Event: Uses his smarts to steal credit cards
Explanation: He gains money and thrills at substantial financial harm to strangers.

Event: Uses his smarts to fake tests for others for pay
Explanation: He profits and they pass, but it corrodes academic integrity and risks sanctions.

Event: Intends to sell the Gallagher house despite younger siblings living there
Explanation: He seeks financial/strategic gain while making his siblings potentially homeless.

Event: Tells all siblings to prepare to find their own places
Explanation: He offloads responsibility and pushes them out, causing stress and risk for them.

Event: Fails to remember Liam is only ten and dependent on older siblings
Explanation: His neglectful planning endangers a child without providing him clear benefit.

Event: Adopts Liam after realizing the homelessness risk
Explanation: Taking legal/parental responsibility helps Liam greatly and grounds Lip as a parent.

Event: Warns the social worker about dangers of placing Karen’s baby in a bad home
Explanation: He spends effort to ensure the baby’s safety, mainly benefiting the child.

Event: Insists on finding a safe, loving adoptive environment for the baby
Explanation: He pushes for a good home with little personal gain but high benefit to the child.

Event: Rejects Mandy semi-moving in and doing his laundry
Explanation: He protects his autonomy while hurting her feelings and relationship hopes.

Event: Assaults Helene’s son, thinking he’s a rival
Explanation: Driven by jealousy he risks serious trouble for dubious emotional “relief” and harms the boy.

Event: Goes to Helene’s house after breakup, drunkenly demanding she come out
Explanation: His outburst jeopardizes her career and his reputation with no real gain.

Event: Develops fixation where he can’t get aroused unless thinking of Helene
Explanation: Obsession restricts his own functioning while not clearly affecting others positively.

Event: Smashes up several cars in a drunken rage
Explanation: He destroys property, gets caught, and gains nothing.

Event: Gets caught by campus security and is expelled
Explanation: Expulsion devastates his prospects and also wastes institutional resources.

Event: Tells Fiona he’ll inevitably get a much better job and support the family
Explanation: Boosts his ego and perhaps offers hope while demeaning her sacrifices.

Event: Searches for pedophiles after man masturbates in front of Debbie
Explanation: He risks himself to protect neighborhood kids from sexual predators.

Event: Gathers neighborhood men to go beat up the identified offender
Explanation: He organizes vigilante violence that may protect kids but bypasses lawful process.

Event: Suggests they still “do something” about the attractive female offender
Explanation: He pushes for punitive action partly from principle but with questionable motives and due process.

Event: Works hard at college and part-time jobs while contributing to family
Explanation: His effort improves his future and provides financial and practical support at home.

Event: Engages in embarrassing drinking blackouts and hospitalizations
Explanation: Alcohol abuse damages his health and relationships without any offsetting benefits.

Event: Has an affair with Helene (older professor)
Explanation: He gains sex and excitement while endangering her career and ethical boundaries.

Event: Lives in a sorority and uses a photo of Helene for arousal
Explanation: He navigates his hang-ups enough to resume sex, benefiting himself and partners without obvious harm.

Event: Loses his appeal to get back into college
Explanation: His prior actions result in a failed appeal, severely hurting him with little broader effect.

Event: Fully relapses into alcoholism after appeal loss and breakup with Sierra
Explanation: Relapse deepens his problems and strains those around him.

Event: Sobers up again by end of Season 7 and attends AA regularly
Explanation: Sustained sobriety benefits him and stabilizes his relationships.

Event: Steps up as surrogate father when Fiona goes off the rails
Explanation: Taking over parenting duties is costly but greatly protects and supports the kids.

Event: Takes Liam in to live with him, Tami, and Fred when house is sold
Explanation: He prevents Liam’s homelessness and builds a stable family unit for them both.

Event: Sleeps with many women over the series
Explanation: He gains pleasure; impact on others is mixed but includes hurt feelings and complications.

Event: Refuses to accept apologies or reconciliation attempts from Monica
Explanation: He protects his own boundaries and avoids pain while blocking any chance of her growth or reconnection.

Event: Finishes high school and gets into college (with Mandy’s help)
Explanation: Educational progress improves his prospects and offers hope to his family.

Event: Goes on drunken benders and “Frank-like” ranting in Season 6
Explanation: His spirals hurt his health and family relationships without any upside.

Event: Wakes up on a train from a drunken stupor
Explanation: He risks safety and dignity for no discernible benefit.

Event: Prefers carcinogens (booze, smoking) over stimulants like ecstasy
Explanation: Choosing harmful substances damages his health while not particularly helping others.

Event: Stalks Karen after she dumps him
Explanation: Obsessive behavior disturbs her and degrades him with no positive outcome.

Event: Ignores Mandy’s feelings at every turn
Explanation: He preserves his freedom and ego while repeatedly hurting her emotionally.

Event: Demands the family sell the Gallagher house though he doesn’t live there
Explanation: He seeks a payout/control at significant cost and risk to siblings still relying on the house.

Event: Steals from his former workplace to satiate his ego despite promising Tami to stop
Explanation: He feeds his ego briefly while risking legal trouble and betraying Tami’s trust.

Event: Brags to a waiting student (the future tutor) about scamming extra credit
Explanation: He aims to game the system for personal gain while devaluing the tutor’s work.

Event: Tries to invite the tutor to a party and assumes she’ll want to hook up
Explanation: He seeks sex and status while objectifying her and risking social backlash.

Event: Assumes the tutor will want to take him back to her apartment
Explanation: He inflates his ego with expectations that ignore her autonomy, minor social harm to her.

Event: Tells an older girl at the party that his planned hookup failed and assumes she’ll ditch to hook up with him
Explanation: He chases gratification while showing disregard for her perspective, provoking her anger.

Event: Works as a delivery boy while struggling to find a house for his family
Explanation: Low-status work and housing search are hard but directly support Tami and the kids.

Event: Feels mostly happy about Tami’s second pregnancy despite financial strain
Explanation: Optimism and acceptance strengthen family bonds though it adds pressure.

Event: Refuses to commit to Mandy or Karen when they want relationships
Explanation: He safeguards his autonomy while causing them emotional pain and instability.

Event: Gets more hostile in Season 7, resenting his family’s success
Explanation: Bitterness harms relationships and his own mental state without bringing real benefits.
